title: An Analysis of Temporal Dropout in Earth Observation Time Series for Regression Tasks
keywords: Dropout Earth Observation Time Series Regression Uncertainty Quantification
main_topic: Handling missing data in Earth Observation time series data for regression tasks with focus on uncertainty quantification
problem: Missing data in time series sensor data in Earth Observation applications, caused by sensor anomalies and occlusions (e.g., clouds in optical images)
solutions: Using dropout as an augmentation technique during training
Using dropout for uncertainty quantification during inference
Learning optimal dropout ratios using Concrete Dropout instead of manual tuning
datasets: Three Earth Observation datasets with temporal sensor data (not specifically named in the provided text)
results: MC-ConcTD improves predictive accuracy and uncertainty calibration while eliminating the need for manual dropout tuning
